QF.id: XXVA Introduction to Research Analyst Framework_16

question.text: Section 11 of the SEBI Act, 1992 empowers SEBI to take all measures to:

answer.text: Only register intermediaries in the securities market
Protect interests of investors in securities and promote development of and regulate the securities market
Only conduct inspections and audits of stock exchanges
Only impose penalties on defaulting intermediaries

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Section 11(1) of the SEBI Act provides SEBI with broad powers — to protect investor interests, promote development of, and regulate the securities market by such measures as it thinks fit. This is SEBI's omnibus power. **The Trap:** Registration, inspection, and penalties are all individual measures under this broad mandate — Section 11 is much wider than any single function. **Cross-Exam Context:** This is tested across all NISM exams as the foundational power of SEBI. Series III-A and III-C cover SEBI's regulatory powers in detail. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Section 11's broad language ('such measures as it thinks fit') gives SEBI significant regulatory flexibility — stay updated on new circulars and guidelines as SEBI actively uses this power to address emerging market issues.
